##NAMES=Plumbogaidonnayite
##RRUFFID=R250045
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=PbZrSi_3_O_9_·2H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Saima alkaline complex, Liaoning Province, China
##OWNER=Geological Museum of China
##SOURCE=Bin Wu
##DESCRIPTION=Aggregates of subhedral to anhedral or platy transparent, colourless crystals associated closely with microcline, natrolite, aegirine, gaidonnayite, georgechaoite, zircon, bobtraillite and britholite-(Ce). Type sample described in Wu et al (2024)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250045
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Pb_0.70_Ca_0.17_Ba_0.01_K_0.11_Na_0.01_Y_0.01_)(Zr_1.00_Hf_0.01_Ti_0.01_)Si_3.01_O_9_·2H_2_O
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Grains, in Gandolfi mode
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 11.7696 b: 13.0048 c: 6.6588 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 90 volume: 1019.21 crystal system: orthorhombic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
